messages.content: You are helping build OpenChronicle, a modular interactive storytelling engine that runs locally or in Docker. Users create structured storypacks containing canon, characters, memory, and meta.yaml config files. The system loads these packs, tracks scenes in SQLite databases, builds prompts for LLMs, and supports rollback, memory mutation, and canonical continuity. Code must remain lightweight and portable, optimized for Raspberry Pi, NAS, or cloud-based hosting.

Project Structure:
- /core/: main Python modules (loader, memory, context builder, logger, rollback, database)
- /storypacks/: per-story folders with canon, characters, memory, and style guides
- /storage/: SQLite databases per story, with exports folder

Current modules (SQLite-based):
- story_loader.py: loads storypacks and parses meta.yaml
- scene_logger.py: logs scenes to SQLite with flags, memory snapshots, rollback support
- context_builder.py: builds prompts using current memory and canon snippets
- memory_manager.py: manages character memory, world state, flags, recent events via SQLite
- rollback_engine.py: complete rollback system with backup and integrity validation
- database.py: SQLite utilities, migration, stats, and table management
- model_adapter.py: unified LLM adapter system (OpenAI, Ollama, mock) with async support

Architecture:
- Each story gets its own SQLite database at storage/{story_id}/openchronicle.db
- Tables: scenes, memory, memory_history, rollback_points, rollback_backups
- Static content (canon, characters) remains in YAML/text files
- Dynamic data (scenes, memory, rollback) stored in SQLite
- Model adapters configured via config/models.json
- CLI interface with model switching and management

Completed features:
- ✅ SQLite integration with all core modules
- ✅ Model adapter system with OpenAI, Ollama, mock support
- ✅ Enhanced CLI with model management commands
- ✅ Comprehensive test suite (9 tests passing)
- ✅ Configuration management system
- ✅ Interaction logging for debugging

Next priorities:
- Enhanced CLI interface with better UX
- Export system for user logs (PDF, ePub, txt)
- API endpoints for external integration
- Web interface for browser-based interaction
- Advanced memory features (semantic search, compression)
- Story templates and genre packs

Instructions:
- Don't hardcode file paths
- Keep each module focused and clean
- Think in story logic, not web app logic
- Assume multiple LLM backend support
- Maintain SQLite-based architecture for performance
